reason: Classic Korean abalone porridge spot beloved by locals. Comforting and nourishing — a perfect light meal.
Hand-cut noodle soup and bossam in a traditional market vibe. Authentic and hearty.
Warm and rustic kalguksu joint — thick handmade noodles in rich broth with bossam on the side.
Seoul institution for kongguksu (soybean noodle soup). A must-try summer classic since 1963.

name_en: Manjok Ohyang Jokbal, City Hall Branch
reason: Five-spice braised pig feet — crispy outside, tender inside. A beloved late-night Seoul staple.
Roasted whole chicken with a Korean twist. Juicy and flavorful, perfect with a cold beer.
Korean fine dining experience rooted in royal court cuisine. Elegant presentation and deep flavors.
Iconic samgyetang restaurant — ginseng chicken soup in a traditional hanok setting near Gyeongbokgung.
One of Seoul's oldest restaurants (est. 1904). Rich milky ox-bone soup — a true taste of history.
Handmade dumplings with thin skin and generous filling. A hidden local favorite.
Artisan jambon-beurre sandwiches in the Anguk neighborhood. Simple, fresh, and perfectly made.
Old-school Korean snack shop for tuna kimbap and tteokbokki. Nostalgic and delicious street food.
Wood-fired pizza with Korean craft. Crispy thin crust and creative toppings in a cozy setting.

name_en: Hongji Roast｜Korean BBQ｜グルメ ホンーじ ロース
reason: Sizzling samgyeopsal BBQ with quality pork cuts. Fun, lively Korean BBQ atmosphere.
Korea's signature fried chicken chain — crispy soy-garlic glaze. The Jongno flagship branch.
Modern Korean restaurant with creative interpretations of traditional dishes. Stylish and flavorful.
Tender Korean BBQ pork belly served thick-cut. Great quality meat with classic side dishes.
Legendary gomtang (beef bone soup) since 1939. Rich, cloudy broth that defines Seoul comfort food.
Fun fusion taco spot blending Korean and Mexican flavors. Casual and creative.
Hearty kalguksu with crispy pajeon (green onion pancake). Homestyle and satisfying.
Premium bulgogi stew in a refined setting. Tender marinated beef with rich, savory broth.
Modern Korean home-style meals with a garden café atmosphere. Warm and inviting.
Rich and hearty sundae gukbap (blood sausage soup). A beloved Euljiro local staple.
Artisan Italian with fresh handmade pasta and bread. A polished dining experience.
Craft fermented pizza and natural beer in a charming Seochon alley. Unique and delicious.
Hearty puffer fish soup known for its clean, warming broth. A specialty from Korea's south coast.
Italian-Korean fusion in a refined atmosphere. Creative dishes with quality ingredients.
Authentic Thai cuisine in the heart of Seoul. Fresh flavors and vibrant dishes.
Upscale Korean dining paired with natural wines. An intimate and sophisticated experience.
Clay pot rice (sotbap) cooked to order with seasonal toppings. Crispy nurungji at the bottom is the highlight.
Spicy braised chicken stew bursting with flavor. A local comfort food classic.
Refined Korean set menu dining with seasonal fish dishes. Elegant and carefully curated.
Simple, honest kalguksu — handmade noodles in a rich anchovy broth. Pure comfort.
Korean set menu in a beautifully restored hanok. Traditional atmosphere with quality dishes.
Premium kalguksu with a clean, deep broth. One of the best noodle experiences in Seoul.
Century-old tofu restaurant. Silky fresh tofu and clean-flavored Korean dishes.
Oyster rice and oyster soup from fresh seasonal oysters. A cozy winter gem.
Elegant kalguksu near Deoksugung Palace. A warm bowl in a historically rich neighborhood.
Nostalgic tteokbokki with a homemade taste. A sweet and spicy street-food classic done right.
Tender boiled pork bossam with fresh kimchi wraps. A timeless Korean drinking-food pairing.
Charming hanok café tucked in Chungmuro. Traditional Korean architecture meets modern café culture.
A hidden hanok café with European-inspired pastries. Quiet courtyard seating in a traditional house.

subcategory: Korean Tea House (Jeju green tea and refined tea desserts)
reason: Jeju green tea and refined tea desserts in a beautifully designed space. Korea's tea culture at its best.

subcategory: A stylish European-inspired café near Namsan with quality desserts and coffee.
reason: A stylish European-inspired café near Namsan with quality desserts and sweeping city views.
Rooftop coffee lounge with expansive city views. Industrial chic meets panoramic Seoul skyline.
Iconic bakery café in a renovated hanok. Famous for pastries and a beautiful fusion of old and new.
A café with a direct view overlooking Changdeokgung Palace. History and coffee in one serene spot.

subcategory: A panoramic views of the Han River
reason: Panoramic Han River views from a spacious modern café. Perfect for sunset watching.

subcategory: A panoramic view of Inwangsan mountain
reason: Quirky hilltop café with a retro amusement park theme and sweeping Inwangsan mountain views.

subcategory: A refined hanok lounge where each group enjoys their own quiet space
reason: A refined hanok tea lounge where each group enjoys their own quiet space. The most serene café in Bukchon.

subcategory: A panoramic views of the Euljiro and Jongno
reason: Rooftop café with sweeping views across Euljiro and Jongno. A hidden viewpoint locals love.

subcategory: A nature-surrounded café on a hillside, offering open view of Inwangsan mountain
reason: Hillside café surrounded by nature with wide-open views of Inwangsan mountain. Breathtaking on a clear day.

subcategory: Open views of Namsan and central Seoul
reason: Café near Namsan Library with open views of central Seoul. At 9 PM, all lights go off for a stunning night view.
A sophisticated rooftop bar in Gangnam overlooking northern Seoul. Dramatic skyline and cocktails.

subcategory: A classic hotel bar with a timeless, elegant mood
reason: A classic hotel bar with timeless elegance and a sweeping view of the city from Namsan hillside.
Refined speakeasy cocktail bar inside Four Seasons Seoul. World-class mixology in an intimate setting.

subcategory: A true Euljiro-style bar with a raw, local vibe
reason: A raw, authentic Euljiro-style bar with local character. The real deal for Seoul nightlife culture.

A traditional Korean hanok house turned fine-dining restaurant with a small courtyard. Originally the historic home of legendary dancer Choe Seung-hui. The refined, authentic Korean course meals never disappoint — even a foreign friend loved it so much she returned on her next Seoul trip.

Famous for marinated soy crab (ganjang-gejang) course in the quiet Seongbuk-dong neighborhood. Elegant Korean cuisine in a serene setting. Nearby 'Seonjam' café and the legendary 'Seongbuk-dong Nurungji Baeksuk' are within walking distance.

A 500-year-old historic estate built by a descendant of King Sejong's fifth son. The largest-scale hanok hanjeongsik experience — each group is served in their own private hanok room. The spacious traditional garden is perfect for a stroll. Nearby: 'Singmulgwan PH', a botanical garden-themed café.

Run by a certified artisan (Intangible Cultural Heritage holder) of royal Korean confections. Every rice cake and hangwa is genuinely delicious. Don't miss the injeolmi croissant near the counter. Beautiful interior and a lovely terrace in spring. Enter from the side stairway, not the front.

The most serene hanok café in Bukchon. Each group gets their own private room with tea served on a traditional tray. The stone-wall-facing toenmaru (wooden porch) seats are magical. Unlike most noisy Bukchon cafés, this one is genuinely quiet. Tip: if floor seating is uncomfortable, choose the toenmaru instead.

Perched high in Buam-dong with a wide-open view of Inwangsan mountain. Foreigners are often amazed that Seoul — a capital city — is surrounded by mountains. On a clear day, the panoramic view here is breathtaking.

A café near Namsan Library with stunning city views. The highlight: at exactly 9 PM, all interior lights are turned off, and the Seoul night skyline comes alive through the windows. A truly romantic moment for those seeking it.

Located on the 22nd floor of Courtyard Marriott Seoul Myeongdong, this café serves afternoon tea on najeonchilgi (mother-of-pearl lacquerware) gujeolpan plates. Panoramic views of Namsan Tower, Namdaemun Gate, and the traditional market below. Reservation required for the afternoon tea set.
